---
title: "Themes"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(tabviz)
```

Built-in theme presets, the `web_theme()` custom-theme constructor, the four core
modifiers (`set_inputs()`, `set_variants()`, `set_spacing()`, `set_theme_field()`),
and `package_themes()` for listing every shipped theme. Pass any theme to
`tabviz(theme = ...)` or apply it after the fact with
[`set_theme()`](styling.qmd#set_theme).

For the conceptual model see the [Themes guide](../guide/themes.qmd) and the
[Styling Cascade](../concepts/styling-cascade.qmd) concepts page.

---

## Preset themes

`web_theme_*()` builders return a fully resolved `WebTheme`. They take no
arguments — chain modifiers to customize. Seven presets ship: four
**journal** themes (restraint-led, designed for publication) and three
**LOTR** themes (illustrative, exercise the full surface for talks /
blog posts / themed reports).

| Theme | Source | Purpose | Notable defaults |
|---|---|---|---|
| [`web_theme_cochrane()`](#web_theme_cochrane) | `R/themes.R` | The package default | Cochrane teal + warm-coral accent, Inter sans-serif, comfortable density |
| [`web_theme_lancet()`](#web_theme_lancet)     | `R/themes.R` | Lancet-style editorial | Navy + refined old-gold + warm cream, Georgia serif |
| [`web_theme_jama()`](#web_theme_jama)         | `R/themes.R` | JAMA / print journals | Pure black-and-white, ultra-compact, strong dividers, Arial |
| [`web_theme_dark()`](#web_theme_dark)         | `R/themes.R` | Dark-mode viewing | Catppuccin Mocha-inspired canvas, pastel markers |
| [`web_theme_dwarven()`](#web_theme_dwarven)   | `R/themes-lotr.R` | Talks, themed reports | Stone neutrals, forge-glow accent, broad serif display |
| [`web_theme_elvish()`](#web_theme_elvish)     | `R/themes-lotr.R` | Talks, themed reports | Twilight surface, mithril silvers, calligraphic serif |
| [`web_theme_hobbit()`](#web_theme_hobbit)     | `R/themes-lotr.R` | Talks, themed reports | Parchment + green, hand-set body face, hearth accent |

```r
tabviz(data, label = "study",
       columns = list(viz_forest(point = "hr", lower = "lo", upper = "hi")),
       theme = web_theme_lancet())
```

### `web_theme_cochrane()` {#web_theme_cochrane}
### `web_theme_lancet()` {#web_theme_lancet}
### `web_theme_jama()` {#web_theme_jama}
### `web_theme_dark()` {#web_theme_dark}
### `web_theme_dwarven()` {#web_theme_dwarven}
### `web_theme_elvish()` {#web_theme_elvish}
### `web_theme_hobbit()` {#web_theme_hobbit}

All preset builders take no arguments and return a fully resolved `WebTheme`.

---

## `package_themes()` {#package_themes}

Returns a 2-level named list of every theme distributed with the package
(`journals` and `lotr`) — handy for the `enable_themes` argument of
[`web_interaction()`](interaction.qmd) or for iterating in tests. The
nested shape is preserved by the in-widget switcher when you pass it
through, producing a tabbed picker (one tab per category).

```r
package_themes() |> str(max.level = 2)
# List of 2
#  $ journals:List of 4
#   ..$ cochrane: <WebTheme>
#   ..$ lancet  : <WebTheme>
#   ..$ jama    : <WebTheme>
#   ..$ dark    : <WebTheme>
#  $ lotr    :List of 3
#   ..$ dwarven: <WebTheme>
#   ..$ elvish : <WebTheme>
#   ..$ hobbit : <WebTheme>
```

---

## `web_theme()` {#web_theme}

Custom-theme constructor. Extends a `base_theme` with named lists of
Tier 1 inputs and per-table variants. Resolves the theme before returning.

```r
web_theme(
  name = "custom",
  inputs = NULL,        # Named list of ThemeInputs property overrides
  variants = NULL,      # Named list of ThemeVariants property overrides
  base_theme = web_theme_cochrane()
)
```

| Argument | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | `"custom"` | Theme name (shown in the in-widget switcher and in source export). |
| `inputs` | named list | `NULL` | Tier 1 input overrides. Any `ThemeInputs` field is valid: `neutral`, `primary` / `primary_deep`, `secondary` / `secondary_deep`, `accent` / `accent_deep`, `status_positive` / `status_negative` / `status_warning` / `status_info`, `series_anchors`, `slot_style`, `font_body` / `font_display` / `font_mono`. Unset fields inherit from `base_theme`. (Legacy `brand` accepted with deprecation warning; rewrite as `primary`.) |
| `variants` | named list | `NULL` | Per-table variant overrides: `density` (`compact` / `comfortable` / `spacious`), `header_style` (`light` / `tint` / `bold`), `first_column_style` (`default` / `bold`). |
| `base_theme` | `WebTheme` | `web_theme_cochrane()` | Starting point. The `inputs` and `variants` lists are applied on top. |
| `.validate` | logical | `TRUE` | Run construction-time contrast validation on the resolved theme. |

Example — Lancet-inspired theme with bold headers:

```r
custom <- web_theme(
  name = "navy-bold",
  inputs = list(
    primary          = "#1F3A5F",
    primary_deep     = "#0F2138",
    accent         = "#B08938",
    series_anchors = c("#1F3A5F", "#B08938", "#6B8E7F"),
    font_body      = "Charter, Georgia, serif"
  ),
  variants = list(density = "comfortable", header_style = "bold")
)
```

---

## Theme modifiers

All modifiers operate on a `WebTheme` and return a new (re-resolved) `WebTheme`.
Chainable via the pipe.

### `set_inputs()` {#set_inputs}

Update Tier 1 customer-facing inputs. Cascade-aware: changing `primary`
clears `primary_deep` so it re-derives; changing `series_anchors` rebuilds
the slot bundles. The pooled-effect diamond reads from `series[1]`, so
anchoring it is just a matter of setting the first `series_anchors` entry.

```r
set_inputs(
  theme,
  neutral,
  primary, primary_deep, secondary, secondary_deep,
  accent, accent_deep,
  status_positive, status_negative, status_warning, status_info,
  series_anchors,
  slot_style,
  font_body, font_display, font_mono
)
```

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `theme` | `WebTheme` | Theme to modify |
| `neutral` | `chr[5]` | 5-step ramp, lightest → darkest |
| `primary` | hex | Primary identity (markers, accents) |
| `primary_deep` | hex | Dark companion for text / strokes / bold-mode header band. Auto-derives by darkening `primary` when `NA` |
| `secondary` | hex | Secondary identity. Owns chrome texture (`surface.muted`, dividers, banding) and structural defaults (column-group bold band, row-group L1/L2/L3 bg, glyph-column defaults). Mirrors `primary` when `NA` |
| `secondary_deep` | hex | Dark companion for `secondary`. Mirrors `secondary`'s deep companion when `NA` |
| `accent` | hex | Engagement layer — hover, selected, semantic row callouts, selection edge. Orthogonal to identity |
| `accent_deep` | hex | Dark companion; defaults to `accent` |
| `status_positive`, `status_negative`, `status_warning`, `status_info` | hex | Semantic status colors |
| `series_anchors` | `chr[]` | Per-effect anchor colors (variable length). The first anchor also drives the pooled-effect diamond |
| `slot_style` | `"fill_with_darker_stroke"` (default) / `"flat_fill"` / `"outlined"` | How each `SlotBundle`'s fill / stroke pair relate |
| `font_body` | string | Primary CSS font-family |
| `font_display` | string | Optional title face; mirrors `font_body` when `NA` |
| `font_mono` | string | Optional monospace face |

### `set_variants()` {#set_variants}

Flip per-table toggles. Density change resets spacing tokens so the new
preset's values flow through.

```r
set_variants(theme, density, header_style, first_column_style)
```

| Argument | Values | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `density` | `"compact"` / `"comfortable"` / `"spacious"` | Drives every spacing token |
| `header_style` | `"light"` / `"tint"` / `"bold"` | Light header on muted surface, primary-tinted band, or bold header on `primary_deep` |
| `first_column_style` | `"default"` / `"bold"` | Excel-style emphasized first column when `"bold"` |

### `set_spacing()` {#set_spacing}

Per-token spacing override on top of the active density preset. Set what you
care about; the rest cascade from density.

```r
set_spacing(
  theme,
  row_height, header_height, padding, container_padding, axis_gap,
  column_group_padding, row_group_padding, cell_padding_x,
  footer_gap, title_subtitle_gap, bottom_margin, indent_per_level
)
```

All values are numeric (pixels).

::: {.callout-note}
## Live aspect-driven `row_height` in the widget

When the in-widget [aspect-ratio slider](../guide/interactivity.qmd#aspect-ratio-slider) is engaged, the lever ladder scales `row_height` to absorb the height delta. The Spacing settings tab reads the **effective** (post-lever-ladder) value, banner-flagged "Row height (aspect)". Editing the field clears the aspect target so the user-typed value pins. See [Themes guide — aspect-aware spacing](../guide/themes.qmd#aspect-aware-spacing).

Other tokens (`header_height`, gaps, paddings) keep the value `set_spacing()` writes; only `row_height` is live-driven by the slider.
:::

### `set_theme_field()` {#set_theme_field}

Generic deep setter. Walks the theme tree by `path` and replaces the leaf
with `value`, then re-resolves. Use this for any field not covered by the
typed setters above.

```r
set_theme_field(theme, path, value)
```

| Argument | Description |
|---|---|
| `theme` | A `WebTheme` |
| `path` | Character or mixed character/integer vector. Strings are property names; integers index list-typed properties (currently only `series`). |
| `value` | New value for the leaf field |

Examples:

```r
# Pin row-group L1 background regardless of theme
theme |> set_theme_field(c("row_group", "L1", "bg"), "#EEEEEE")

# Override series 2's stroke without touching anything else
theme |> set_theme_field(c("series", 2L, "stroke"), "#444444")

# Set Tier 1 inputs surgically
theme |> set_theme_field(c("inputs", "primary"), "#FF0000")

# Tweak axis config
theme |> set_theme_field(c("axis", "gridlines"), TRUE)
theme |> set_theme_field(c("axis", "tick_count"), 5)

# Pin layout config
theme |> set_theme_field(c("layout", "container_border_radius"), 4)
```

Re-resolution preserves explicit overrides on subsequent edits.
`set_inputs(primary = "...")` after pinning `surface.base` will refresh
series colors but leave `surface.base` pinned.

---

## Switching themes on a spec

| Verb | Operates on | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| [`set_theme()`](styling.qmd#set_theme) | `WebSpec`, htmlwidget, proxy | Swap to a preset name or `WebTheme` object |
| [`selectable_themes()`](styling.qmd) | `WebSpec`, htmlwidget | Curate the in-widget theme switcher |

```r
# Apply by name
plot |> set_theme("jama")

# Apply by object
custom <- web_theme(inputs = list(primary = "#1F3A5F"))
plot |> set_theme(custom)
```

---

## Worked examples

### Lancet-style with custom Tier 1 input

```r
my_lancet <- web_theme(
  name = "my-lancet",
  inputs = list(
    primary          = "#0066CC",
    accent         = "#CC9900",
    series_anchors = c("#0066CC", "#CC9900", "#669933"),
    font_body      = "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif",
    font_display   = "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif"
  ),
  variants = list(density = "comfortable")
)

# Swap presets, then tweak
custom <- web_theme_lancet() |>
  set_inputs(primary = "#0066CC") |>
  set_variants(density = "compact") |>
  set_theme_field(c("axis", "gridlines"), TRUE)
```

### High-contrast print theme

```r
print_theme <- web_theme(
  name = "print",
  inputs = list(
    neutral        = c("#FFFFFF", "#FFFFFF", "#FAFAFA", "#666666", "#1A1A1A"),
    primary          = "#000000",
    accent         = "#000000",
    series_anchors = c("#1A1A1A", "#4D4D4D", "#808080", "#B3B3B3"),
    font_body      = "'Times New Roman', serif",
    font_display   = "'Times New Roman', serif"
  ),
  variants = list(density = "compact"),
  base_theme = web_theme_jama()
) |>
  set_theme_field(c("row_group", "L1", "text", "weight"), 700) |>
  set_theme_field(c("row_group", "L1", "border_bottom"), TRUE)
```

### Dark dashboard theme

```r
dark_theme <- web_theme(
  name = "dashboard-dark",
  base_theme = web_theme_dark(),
  inputs = list(
    primary          = "#10B981",
    accent         = "#10B981",
    series_anchors = c("#10B981", "#3B82F6", "#F59E0B", "#EF4444")
  ),
  variants = list(density = "spacious", header_style = "bold")
)
```

---

## Theme structure (S7)

A `WebTheme` is an S7 object with the following top-level properties.
For the conceptual model and how the cascades compose, see
[Styling Cascade](../concepts/styling-cascade.qmd) and
[S7 Architecture](../technical/s7-classes.qmd).

| Property | Type | Tier | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | — | Theme identifier |
| `inputs` | `ThemeInputs` | 1 | Customer-facing inputs |
| `variants` | `ThemeVariants` | 1 | Per-table toggles |
| `surface` | `Surfaces` | 2 | base / muted / raised |
| `content` | `Content` | 2 | primary / secondary / muted / inverse |
| `divider` | `Dividers` | 2 | subtle / strong |
| `accent` | `AccentRoles` | 2 | default / muted / tint_subtle / tint_medium |
| `status` | `StatusColors` | 2 | positive / negative / warning / info |
| `series` | `list<SlotBundle>` | 2 | Per-anchor 7-field bundle |
| `summary` | `SlotBundle` | 2 | Pooled-effect diamond bundle |
| `text` | `TextRoles` | 2 | 8 named role bundles |
| `spacing` | `SpacingTokens` | 2 | 12 density-derived numerics |
| `annotation` | `AnnotationCluster` | 3 | title / subtitle / caption / footnote |
| `header` | `HeaderCluster` | 3 | light + bold variants |
| `column_group` | `ColumnGroupCluster` | 3 | Column-group header |
| `row_group` | `RowGroupCluster` | 3 | L1 / L2 / L3 tiers |
| `row` | `RowCluster` | 3 | base / alt / hover / selected + the five semantic bundles (`emphasis`, `muted`, `accent`, `bold`, `fill`) |
| `cell` | `CellCluster` | 3 | bg / fg / border / text |
| `first_column` | `FirstColumnCluster` | 3 | plain + bold variants |
| `plot` | `PlotScaffold` | 3 | axis / tick / gridline / reference + mark sizes |
| `marks` | `MarksRecipes` | 3 | Per-mark slot-bundle recipes |
| `axis` | `AxisConfig` | config | Forest x-axis range/ticks/grid |
| `layout` | `Layout` | config | Plot width, container border |
